articles.title: Soldier Busted for Betting on Secret Mission
articles.summary: A U.S. soldier who helped plan a secret mission to capture Venezuela's president was arrested for using secret info to bet on the mission. He won over $400,000 on a betting website called Polymarket. Now people are worried about insider trading (using secret info to make money). President Trump said he doesn't like betting on world events. The White House warned its staff not to use secret info to bet on the war with Iran. Some critics say Trump's son works for betting companies, which might be a problem.

articles.title: Old Carousel Gets a New Spin — and a Civil Rights Story
articles.summary: A carousel on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., reopened after three years of repairs. But this isn't just any merry-go-round. It used to be at an amusement park that was for white people only. In the 1960s, Black kids and white activists protested to let everyone ride. On August 28, 1963 — the same day Martin Luther King Jr. gave his "I Have a Dream" speech — the park opened to all. Now the carousel is a symbol of freedom. Kids today love it — one boy said it's the fastest carousel he's ever been on!

articles.title: Ships Caught in the Middle: US and Iran Seize Vessels, Breaking Rules
articles.summary: A big shipping group says the US and Iran are breaking international law by capturing each other's ships. Iran seized two ships, and the US captured one. The group says innocent sailors are being used as pawns in a political fight. About 20,000 seafarers are stuck in the Gulf because of the conflict. The Strait of Hormuz, a key waterway for oil, is nearly closed. This has made fuel prices go up around the world. The shipping group says all countries should respect freedom of navigation.
